pkg/kafka/metrics.purpose: Top-level OTel metrics facade for librdkafka client statistics: a single Metrics struct holds the top-level Int64Gauges plus broker/topic/consumer-group sub-metric structs from internal/, and Add() walks a parsed stats.Stats (from stats/) recording every gauge. New(meter, ...Option) is the only constructor; wired into app/common, cmd/server, ingest/kafkaingest, and sink.

patterns.name: Single Metrics struct of Int64Gauges + sub-metric pointers
patterns.description: Metrics embeds top-level metric.Int64Gauge fields plus *internal.BrokerMetrics/TopicMetrics/ConsumerGroupMetrics; New populates all of them and returns (*Metrics, error).
patterns.example: type Metrics struct { brokerMetrics *internal.BrokerMetrics; Age metric.Int64Gauge; ... }

patterns.name: New is the only constructor; every gauge creation wraps the error
patterns.description: Each meter.Int64Gauge(...) call is immediately followed by `if err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create metric: <name>: %w", err) }`. Sub-metric constructors wrap with "failed to create <broker|topic|consumer group> metrics: %w".
patterns.example: m.Age, err = meter.Int64Gauge("kafka.age_microseconds", ...); if err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create metric: kafka.age: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Add is nil-safe and attribute-additive
patterns.description: Add(ctx, stats, attrs...) returns early on `stats == nil`, appends name/client_id/type attributes, records every top-level gauge, then dispatches to non-nil sub-metric structs.
patterns.example: func (m *Metrics) Add(ctx context.Context, stats *stats.Stats, attrs ...attribute.KeyValue) { if stats == nil { return } ... }

patterns.name: Functional Options gate sub-metrics
patterns.description: Options{extendedMetrics, brokerMetricsDisabled, topicMetricsDisabled, consumerGroupMetricsDisabled} are set via Option funcs (WithExtendedMetrics, WithBrokerMetricsDisabled, WithTopicMetricsDisabled, WithConsumerGroupMetricsDisabled); disabled sub-metrics stay nil so Add skips them.
patterns.example: func WithExtendedMetrics() Option { return func(o *Options) { o.extendedMetrics = true } }

patterns.name: Bootstrap broker filtering on the facade side
patterns.description: When iterating stats.Brokers, brokers with NodeID < 0 (bootstrap nodes) are skipped before calling brokerMetrics.Add.
patterns.example: for _, broker := range stats.Brokers { if broker.NodeID < 0 { continue }; m.brokerMetrics.Add(ctx, &broker, attrs...) }

patterns.name: kafka.* metric names with description + unit
patterns.description: Every Int64Gauge uses a dotted name (kafka.message_count, kafka.message_size_bytes) plus metric.WithDescription and metric.WithUnit ("{message}", "{byte}", "{microseconds}", "{topic}").

key_file_guides.role: Defines Metrics, Options/Option (+With* funcs), New constructor, and Add recorder; the public surface of the package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A gauge needs THREE coordinated edits: struct field, New() block with the error-wrap guard, and a m.<Field>.Record(...) line in Add — missing any one silently drops the metric. Existing copy-paste artifact: kafka.requests_received_bytes wraps its error string as "kafka.requests-sent"; do not propagate that — use the real metric name in new code.

key_file_guides.role: Table test TestWithMetrics exercising New with each Option combo against a noop meter, plus NewTestStats helper unmarshalling the embedded stats/testdata/stats.json fixture.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses noop.NewMeterProvider() so it only asserts construction/recording does not error, not metric values; uses t.Context(). New Options must be added to the test table or they go untested.

anti_patterns: Adding a gauge to New() without the immediate `if err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create metric: <name>: %w", err) }` guard
Adding a struct field + New() block but forgetting the matching m.<Field>.Record(...) call in Add (or vice versa)
Recording sub-metrics in Add without the `if m.<sub>Metrics != nil` guard — disabled sub-metrics are intentionally nil
Recording bootstrap brokers (NodeID < 0) instead of skipping them
Parsing/computing librdkafka stats here instead of consuming the typed structs from the stats/ sibling package

decisions.decision: Stats parsing (stats/), metric instrument wrappers (internal/), and the recording facade (this package) are split into three layers.
decisions.rationale: Keeps pure-data parsing dependency-light, the internal instrument wrappers reusable per-entity, and this package the single wiring/Add entry point.

decisions.decision: Sub-metrics and extended/percentile metrics are opt-in/opt-out via functional Options.
decisions.rationale: Per-broker/per-topic/per-partition percentile metrics are high-cardinality; callers (sink, ingest) can disable them or enable extended metrics per deployment.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new top-level gauge end-to-end (field + New guard + Record)
